summary: The Docker-based PoC confirms that crafted XML documents cause memory exhaustion in @xmldom/xmldom v0.9.9. Multiple attack vectors successfully demonstrate unbounded resource allocation, with the primary attack vector (1M elements) consuming ~72MB memory and 2.3s processing time.
expected_security_impact: The PoC should generate a malicious XML document, feed it to the parser, and demonstrate measurable resource consumption (memory, CPU time) that satisfies denial-of-service criteria. The verification script confirms that parsing completes successfully with excessive resource usage, proving the vulnerability allows unbounded allocation.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh successfully demonstrated all four DoS attack vectors: (1) Memory exhaustion via 1M elements processed in 2252ms consuming ~72MB, (2) Deep nesting of 5K levels processed in 14ms, (3) Attribute flooding with 10K attributes processed in 219ms, (4) Large 10MB comment triggered stack overflow in 47ms. All attacks completed without triggering any protective limits, confirming unbounded resource allocation vulnerability.
verification_method: The PoC uses Node.js process.memoryUsage() and Date.now() to measure memory consumption and processing time. Success criteria: (1) Memory usage > 50MB for large document attack, (2) Processing time > 1s for large document attack, (3) No errors or limits triggered during parsing, (4) Parser successfully constructs complete DOM tree. The verification distinguishes real DoS from parser errors or input validation rejections.
evidence_produced_by_run: Memory exhaustion attack: 1,000,000 elements processed successfully in 2252ms
Estimated memory consumption: ~72MB for 72MB XML source
Deep nesting attack: 5,000 levels processed without depth limit in 14ms
Attribute flooding: 10,000 attributes processed without count limit in 219ms
Large comment: 10MB comment caused stack overflow error in 47ms
No resource limit checks triggered in any attack vector
Parser constructed complete DOM trees for all malicious inputs
Verification output confirms DoS impact: excessive memory/CPU consumption

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
